title: DC-area forecast: Top-notch July weather today, and nice into the weekend
contentHtml:
Always a human at the helm: Updated around-the-clock by Capital Weather meteorologists.

Happening now: Temperatures rise into the 70s by mid-morning, then perhaps staying there most of the day. Clouds are probably most numerous early before skies become mostly sunny.

What’s next? Pretty similar tomorrow and seemingly through Saturday. Eventually, summer comes back, with more July-like conditions by Monday and Tuesday.

Today’s daily digit — 10/10: Cough, cough. May need the day off. | 🤚 Your call?
The digit is a somewhat subjective rating of the day’s weather, on a 0-to-10 scale.

Forecast in detail

Today (Thursday): Partly to mostly sunny and generally delightful. Humidity falls off with dew points in the 50s and perhaps 40s, which means it's quite dry. Highs mainly in the upper 70s to low 80s with light winds from the north and northwest. Confidence: Medium-High 😎 Nice Day!

Tonight: Comfortable this evening, and almost cool overnight. Lows mainly 60 to 65 with partly to mostly clear skies. Confidence: Medium-High

Hourly Forecast | Pollen, Air & UV

Tomorrow (Friday): Quite similar to today. Lots of sun and below-average highs near 80 to the mid-80s. Winds should be light out of the east. Confidence: Medium-High 😎 Nice Day!

Tomorrow night: Partly to mostly clear skies continue. Lows range across the 60s. Confidence: Medium-High

📬 Get our forecast in your inbox

Our morning forecast email is back! It's included with every level of support — from Free to Sustainer — delivered 7 days a week. Sign up at: capitalweather.com/support-us

A look ahead

At one point, Saturday looked cloudier with some shower chances. That activity increasingly looks to stay south. With partial sun, we should again see highs in a near 80 to mid-80s zone. Confidence: Medium

Somewhat warmer and muggier Sunday. With that, a slight chance of afternoon showers or storms. Highs mainly in the mid-80s. Confidence: Medium

Feeling more like July usually does by Monday and Tuesday. Both feature highs in the upper 80s to around 90 and increased odds of afternoon showers or storms, especially on Tuesday. Confidence: Medium

The public preview period for our Weather Now, Forecast Lab, and Climate Room pages will come to an end this weekend, after which much of the content on those pages will be available exclusively to Friends, Founders, and Sustainers. Become a member to support our work and enjoy unlimited access to our premium content.

DC weather history for July 23

A day of extremes, from destructive 1940 storms with flooding and wind damage to oppressive triple-digit heat in 2011.
